scenario_results.reason: Normal in-scope incident handling stayed tied to ticket, platform, logs, owner action, validation, monitoring, client communication, and completion evidence.
Incomplete tenant scope, environment, client approval, logs, access owner, and retrieval hooks correctly forced an awaiting-evidence exception state.
Legal, editorial, privacy, contractual, and final client signoff requests were refused and routed to adjacent owners.
VAST, pixel, verification, and ad server conflicts produced rework and escalation actions with retest evidence requirements.
Completion without test, rollback, approval, validation, implementation, and retention evidence was refused.
Confidentiality and client-delivery conflict was blocked until approved recipient, channel, scope, data-use authority, and clearance evidence existed.
Consent-string service dependency failure produced a cross-team incident summary with impact, blocked operations, evidence, and owner routing.
OpenRTB and schain change-readiness was held because testing, rollback, client-impact, change-window, and owner approval evidence were missing.
Deal ID, seller ID, schain, sellers.json, ads.txt, bid request, exchange log, and DSP rejection terms were normalized into lane-specific evidence and next action.
Production code, access-control bypass, finance, account, and contractual decisions were rejected or routed to adjacent specialists.
Screenshot-only evidence with missing tenant facts, platform policy, logs, and source context produced low-confidence escalation instead of unsupported diagnosis.
Conflicting tenant runbook, platform guidance, contract terms, and client instruction were preserved and escalated without confident synthesis.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold for ad-tech operations execution, exception handling, completion evidence, client workflow, confidentiality, approval awareness, and authority-boundary handling.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and professional-and-information-services abilities.
Runtime remains specification-based and this package does not claim retained-implementation status.
